claim: File Search rewrites queries, decomposes complex queries into multiple searches, runs keyword and semantic search, and reranks before generation.
limitation: Hosted tool has limitations including deterministic pre-search metadata filtering and structured formats.

claim: Semantic search can miss exact keyword matches while lexical search can miss synonyms and paraphrases; hybrid search combines both.
limitation: Sparse and dense scores require normalization or weighting before production.

evidence: Weaviate vector plus BM25 hybrid search
claim: Hybrid search combines vector search and BM25, then fuses normalized scores into a final ranking.
limitation: Fusion method and alpha weighting depend on corpus and query mix.

claim: Qdrant demonstrates dense retrieval, sparse/BM25-style retrieval, and late-interaction reranking as a practical precision pattern.
limitation: Tutorial is not a neutral benchmark and reranking adds latency/cost.

claim: Contextual chunks, embeddings, BM25-style retrieval, and reranking can reduce retrieval failures in Anthropic's tests.
limitation: Vendor research; results may not transfer directly across corpora, models, and workloads.

claim: A 2026 preprint argues rigidly applying GraphRAG to all queries can reduce accuracy and increase latency, supporting adaptive routing.
limitation: Preprint and emerging evidence; not settled consensus.

claim: Production GenAI apps must account for prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, data/model poisoning, vector and embedding weaknesses, misinformation, and unbounded consumption.
limitation: Broad guidance needs translation into RAG-specific retrieval, permission, and logging decisions.
